verses.text: 'The LORD said unto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool.' Jesus cited this of Himself. David's Lord is the Messiah, exalted to God's right hand to reign till every foe submits.

'The LORD said unto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool.' Jesus cited this of Himself. David's Lord is the Messiah, exalted to God's right hand to reign till every foe submits.
'Thy people shall be willing in the day of thy power,' offering themselves freely in holy beauty. His subjects are volunteers. The reign of Christ is upheld not by force but by hearts gladly given.

'The LORD hath sworn... Thou art a priest for ever after the order of Melchizedek.' Hebrews builds on this. Christ is both King and Priest, His priesthood eternal and unchanging, sworn by God Himself.
The Lord at God's right hand will shatter kings and judge the nations in the day of His wrath. His victory is sure. The same Christ who saves will at last overthrow all who oppose His righteous rule.
